description: In 1997, Boeing acquired McDonnell Douglas in a $13 billion deal that created a global aerospace monopoly. The merger is frequently cited by historians as the turning point where Boeing shifted its culture from 'engineering excellence' to 'shareholder primacy'.

key_events.description: Wall Street cheers the consolidation, sending share prices soaring.
Aerospace unions express early concerns regarding the integration of two disparate cultures.
Regulators approve the deal with little friction, unaware of long-term safety culture impacts.

calibration_notes: Captures the 'short-term market triumph vs long-term institutional decline' dynamic.
